Ayah

وَٱقْصِدْ فِى مَشْيِكَ وَٱغْضُضْ مِن صَوْتِكَ ۚ إِنَّ أَنكَرَ ٱلْأَصْوَٰتِ لَصَوْتُ ٱلْحَمِيرِ (۱۹)
Be modest in your bearing, and lower your voice. Indeed the ungainliest of voices is the donkey’s voice.’ (19)

Key Points

Imam Hassan عليه السلام says: Walking fast diminishes the worth of the believer. (311) We read in a hadith: Raising the voice is permissible when inviting people towards Allah and reciting the Quran. (312)

In the recommendations of Luqman, nine commands, three prohibitions, and seven reasons for these commands and prohibitions are mentioned:

Nine Commands: 1. Kindness to parents. 2. Gratitude towards Allah and parents. 3. Companionship with kindness towards parents. 4. Following the path of the believers and the repentant. 5. Establishing prayer (Salat). 6. Enjoining the good (Ma'ruf). 7. Forbidding the evil (Munkar). 8. Moderation in movement (walking). 9. Lowering the voice when speaking.

Three Prohibitions: 1. Prohibition of polytheism (Shirk). 2. Prohibition of turning away from people (arrogance). 3. Prohibition of walking with arrogance.

Seven Reasons: 1. Because human gratitude is for his own benefit, so be grateful. «و من یشكر فانّما یشكر لنفسه» 2. Because polytheism is a great injustice, so do not commit polytheism. «ان الشرك لظلمٌ عظیم» 3. Because the return of everyone is to Him and you must be accountable, so respect your parents. «اِلىَّ المصیر», «الىّ مرجعكم» 4. Because Allah is aware of everything, so be mindful of your deeds. «ان اللّه لطیف خبیر» 5. Because patience is among the matters of importance, so be patient. «ان ذلك من عزم الامور» 6. Because Allah does not love the arrogant, so do not be arrogant. «انّ اللّه لایحبّ كلّ مختال فخور» 7. Because the worst of voices is the loud voice of donkeys, so do not raise your voice. «انّ انكر الاصوات لصوت الحمیر»

311) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 312) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Islam is a comprehensive religion and has instructions and programs even for walking. «واقصد فى مشیك»
  2. In the Divine religion, beliefs and ethics are presented side by side. «لاتشرك باللّه... واقصد فى مشیك»
  3. Moderation, avoidance of excess and deficiency (extremism), and dignity in walking are the recommendations of the Quran. «واقصد فى مشیك»
  4. We should observe moderation not only in walking but in all affairs. «واقصد فى مشیك»
  5. We should lower our voices, avoid useless shouting, and maintain a soft and calm tone. «واغضض من صوتك»
  6. Shouting and raising the voice is a disliked and reprehensible matter. «واغضض من صوتك انّ انكر الاصوات لصوت الحمیر»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَوْا۟ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَخَّرَ لَكُم مَّا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَأَسْبَغَ عَلَيْكُمْ نِعَمَهُۥ ظَـٰهِرَةً وَبَاطِنَةً ۗ وَمِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ مَن يُجَـٰدِلُ فِى ٱللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ وَلَا هُدًى وَلَا كِتَـٰبٍ مُّنِيرٍ (۲٠)
Do you not see that Allah has disposed for you whatever there is in the heavens and whatever there is in the earth and He has showered upon you His blessings, the outward, and the inward? Yet among the people are those who dispute concerning Allah without any knowledge or guidance or an illuminating scripture. (20)

Key Points

Isbāgh (اِسباغ) means to spread out and expand (or extend). In this Ayah, two types of blessings are mentioned: apparent (ظاهرى) blessings, such as health, sustenance, beauty, and the like; and hidden (باطنى) blessings, such as faith (īmān), gnosis (ma'rifah), tranquility (iṭmīnān), good character (ḥusn khulq), unseen aid (imdad ghaybī), knowledge, innate disposition (fiṭrah), Wilāyah (guardianship), and so on. Perhaps it can be said that the meaning of "knowledge" (علم) in the Ayah is rational argumentation, the meaning of "guidance" (هدایت) is innate (fiṭrī) guidance, and the meaning of "Book" (كتاب) is Divine revelation. [This is mentioned] because some individuals speak about Allah and express opinions without relying on any of these: reason, innate disposition, or revelation.

Messages

1- Disregard for creation and its role in human life leads to reproach and blame. «ألم تروا» (Have you not seen/considered?) Sa'di says:

This multitude of wondrous designs on the door and wall of existence, Whoever does not reflect upon them is but a design upon the wall.

2- All creations are purposeful and exist for the benefit of humanity. «سخّر لكم» (He subjected for you)

3- Man is capable of bringing whatever is in the heavens and the earth under his control. «سخّر لكم»

4- Divine blessings are both extensive and abundant, «اسبغ» (He perfected/extended), accessible to His servants, «علیكم» (upon you), and diverse. «ظاهرة و باطنة» (apparent and hidden)

5- We must not neglect the hidden blessings of Allah or be heedless of them. «ألم تروا... باطنة»

6- Despite the vastness and abundance of blessings, man disputes concerning Allah, and this is a form of ingratitude for the blessing. «اسبغ علیكم نعمه... یجادل فى اللّه» (He perfected His blessings upon you... disputes concerning Allah)

7- Logical debate is good, «و جادلهم بالّتى هى احسن» (and argue with them in a way that is best) (313), but a debate that lacks scientific backing, is far from Divine guidance, and opposes the Book of Allah is worthless. (314) «یجادل فى اللّه بغیر علم و لاهُدىً و لاكتاب منیر» (disputes concerning Allah without knowledge or guidance or an illuminating Book)

8- In the Divine worldview, reason (عقل), innate disposition (فطرت), and revelation (وحى) are the valid sources of knowledge, not anything else. «علمٍ، هدىً، كتابٍ منیر» (knowledge, guidance, an illuminating Book) (313) An-Nahl, 125. (314) Mullā Ṣadrā says in his book Asfār: «تبّاً لفلسفة تخالف الكتاب و السنّة», Perish that philosophy which contradicts the Book and the Sunnah.

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱتَّبِعُوا۟ مَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ قَالُوا۟ بَلْ نَتَّبِعُ مَا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ ءَابَآءَنَآ ۚ أَوَلَوْ كَانَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ يَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَىٰ عَذَابِ ٱلسَّعِيرِ (۲۱)
When they are told, ‘Follow what Allah has sent down,’ they say, ‘No, we will follow what we found our fathers following.’ What! Even if Satan be calling them to the punishment of the Blaze? (21)

Key Points

The word 'قِيلَ' (it was said) is a sign of the intensity of the disbelievers' fanaticism. This means that they show obstinacy and refuse to accept any word of truth, regardless of the speaker, paying no attention to who says it. "قِيلَ لَهُمْ" (it was said to them).

Messages

1- An example of wrongful dispute is fanaticism regarding the false ideas of ancestors. "يُجَادِلُ فِي اللَّهِ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ... قَالُوا بَلْ نَتَّبِعُ مَا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ آبَاءَنَا"

2- Following the revelation and what is from Allah is necessary. "اتَّبِعُوا مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ"

3- By inviting the misguided to the truth, we complete the argument (Hujjah) against them. "قِيلَ لَهُمْ"

4- There are two paths for humanity: A: The path of Allah, "مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ" B: The path of Satan. "الشَّيْطَانُ يَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى عَذَابِ السَّعِيرِ"

5- The beliefs of ancestors influence the destiny of future generations. "وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ آبَاءَنَا"

6- Blind imitation and fanaticism are forbidden. "وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ آبَاءَنَا... أَوَلَوْ كَانَ" (Wrongful familial fanaticism is one of the obstacles to accepting the truth and intellectual growth.)

7- Environment, society, and history are influential in choosing a path. "وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ آبَاءَنَا" (Man must move forward with the light of knowledge, intellect, and revelation, so that wherever he sees deviation in the environment, society, or history, he avoids it.)

8- The logic of truth is the principle, not nationalism or the beliefs and conduct of ancestors. "أَوَلَوْ كَانَ الشَّيْطَانُ..."

9- Satan continuously calls humanity to falsehood. "كَانَ الشَّيْطَانُ يَدْعُوهُمْ"

10- Any path other than revelation is a deviation and leads to the torment of Hellfire. "يَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى عَذَابِ السَّعِيرِ"

11- The ultimate consequence of following the hollow ideas of ancestors is Hellfire. "عَذَابِ السَّعِيرِ"

Ayah

۞ وَمَن يُسْلِمْ وَجْهَهُۥٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ وَهُوَ مُحْسِنٌ فَقَدِ ٱسْتَمْسَكَ بِٱلْعُرْوَةِ ٱلْوُثْقَىٰ ۗ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْأُمُورِ (۲۲)
Whoever surrenders his heart to Allah and is virtuous, has certainly held fast to the firmest handle, and with Allah lies the outcome of all matters. (22)

Key Points

In the Quran, words derived from the root silm have been used 140 times, and words such as salām, Islām, and Muslim come from this root.

In the previous Ayah, we read that a group of people cling to their misguided ancestors and turn towards them; in this Ayah, we read that the righteous and pure cling to Allah the Almighty and turn towards Him.

In the previous Ayah, the invitation of Satan to people and his dragging them towards Hell was discussed; this Ayah considers the path to salvation from Satan's whispers to be submission to Allah and performing good deeds.

Submission to other than Allah is slavery and captivity, but submission to Allah is freedom and growth. As the Quran states: «فمَن اَسلم فاولئك تَحَرَّوا رَشَداً» (315)

Truly, if the entire existence is submitted to Allah, why should we not submit to Him?! The Quran states: «أ فغیر دین اللّه یبغون و له اسلم مَن فى السّموات و الارض» (316) Do you seek other than the religion of Allah, while everyone in the heavens and the earth has submitted to Him?

Allah has issued the command for human submission: «فله اَسلِموا» (317) The Prophet must also submit to Allah: «اُمرت أن أكون أوّل من أسلم» (318)

In this Ayah, He also states: «و مَن یُسلم وجهه الى اللّه و هو محسن فقد استمسك بالعروة الوثقى» Any good-doer who sincerely directs his orientation towards Allah has grasped a firm handle.

In another Ayah, we read: «مَن اَسلم وجهه للّه و هو محسن فله أجره عند ربّه» (319) A good-doer who sincerely submits to Allah has a guaranteed reward with his Lord. Or we read: «و مَن أحسن دیناً ممّن اَسلم وجهه للّه» (320) Who is better in religion than one who has submitted himself to Allah?

For his salvation and victory, man chooses multiple supports and clings to various ropes, such as: power, wealth, position, family, friends, lineage, etc. But all these ropes will eventually break, and these supports will lose their effectiveness. The only thing that is stable, lasting, and a factor for salvation is clinging to the firm divine rope, submitting to Him, and performing righteous deeds.

In narrations, we read: The infallible leaders and the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet علیهم السلام, and love and affection for them, are the firm divine rope and the al-Urwah al-Wuthqā (the firmest handle). «نحن العروة الوثقى» (321), «العروة الوثقى المودّة لآل محمّد» (322)

Messages

1- Submission to Allah is a voluntary matter; there is no compulsion or coercion. «مَن یسلم وجهه»

2- Submission to Allah must be accompanied by action. «یسلم... و هو محسن»

3- Being a good-doer is not enough on its own; sincerity in action is also necessary. «یسلم وجهه... و هو محسن»

4- Spiritual concepts should be expressed through analogies and comparisons to tangible and material things. (Sincere action is likened to a firm rope). «عروة الوثقى»

5- We should choose our path by paying attention to the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and reflecting on the future. «و الى اللّه عاقبة الامور»

6- Creation is all directed towards one direction and one goal. «و الى اللّه عاقبة الامور»

Ayahs

وَمَن كَفَرَ فَلَا يَحْزُنكَ كُفْرُهُۥٓ ۚ إِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ فَنُنَبِّئُهُم بِمَا عَمِلُوٓا۟ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (۲۳)
As for those who are faithless, let their faithlessness not grieve you. To Us will be their return, and We will inform them about what they have done. Indeed Allah knows best what is in the breasts. (23)
نُمَتِّعُهُمْ قَلِيلًا ثُمَّ نَضْطَرُّهُمْ إِلَىٰ عَذَابٍ غَلِيظٍ (۲٤)
We will provide for them for a short time, then We will shove them toward a harsh punishment. (24)

Key Points

In Ayah 23, the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is forbidden from grieving over the disbelief (kufr) of a group of people, and this may be for several reasons: A: They are not worthy enough for you to grieve over them. B: Sorrow over their disbelief distracts you from other tasks. C: Their disbelief does not harm you. D: You did not fall short in conveying the message and admonishing them, so there is no need for you to worry. E: Since the disbelievers will return to Us and have no way to escape, there is no place for sorrow and grief. The precedence of the word «الینا» (to Us) over the word «مَرجعهم» (their return) implies that He alone is the ultimate destination (Marja'). The word «مرجع» (return) also implies that He is the origin (Mabda'), because «مَرجع» means returning to that which was the beginning. Furthermore, «الینا مَرجعهم» is a nominal sentence (jumlat ismiyyah), indicating the certainty and inevitability of the Resurrection (Ma'ad).

Messages

1- Comparing good and bad individuals is one of the ways to gain knowledge and make a choice. «مَن یُسلِم وجهه... و مَن كفر»

The merchandise of disbelief and faith is not without customers Some prefer this, and some prefer that.

2- The Prophet was compassionate toward everyone and suffered even from the deviation and disbelief of the opponents. «فلایحزنك كفرهم»

3- Prophets need divine consolation and comfort. «فلایحزنك كفرهم»

4- The responsibility of religious leaders is to guide the people, not to achieve the result. «و مَن كفر فلا یحزنك كفره»

5- People's disbelief affects their own destiny. «فلایحزنك كفره الینا مرجعهم»

6- Disbelievers should not rejoice, because eventually, the hide will reach the tannery (meaning, accountability is inevitable). «الینا مرجعهم»

7- The ultimate destination (Marja') and the origin (Mabda') of human beings is Allah alone. «الینا مرجعهم»

8- Two things lead to broad-mindedness (sa'at-e sadr) and relieve the believers' anxiety regarding the deviation of others: one is attention to Allah's knowledge, and the other is attention to the Resurrection (Ma'ad). «فلایحزنك كفره الینا مرجعهم... علیم بذات الصّدور» (Now that we know Allah knows everything and the return of everyone is to Him, there is no cause for worry.)

9- On the Day of Judgment, man will face the report of his own performance. «فننبّئهم بما عملوا»

10- Allah knows the intentions and motives we held in our hearts and will reveal them on the Day of Judgment. «فننبّئهم... انّ اللّه علیم بذات الصّدور»

11- Attention to the disgrace of the Day of Judgment can prevent obstinacy and insistence upon disbelief. «فننبّئهم بما عملوا»

12- Let not worldly blessings deceive us, for they are few and insignificant compared to the blessings of the Hereafter. «نمتّعهم قلیلا»

13- We should calculate the value of deeds by considering their ultimate outcome. «نمتّعهم قلیلا ثمّ نضطرّهم الى عذاب غلیظ»

14- The disbelief of the disbelievers today is by choice, «و مَن كَفَر», but their entry into Hell tomorrow is compulsory. «نضطرّهم»

15- Material prosperity is not a sign of felicity (sa'adah). «نمتّعهم قلیلا ثمّ نضطرّهم الى عذاب غلیظ»

Ayah

وَلَئِن سَأَلْتَهُم مَّنْ خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ لَيَقُولُنَّ ٱللَّهُ ۚ قُلِ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ۚ بَلْ أَكْثَرُهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۲٥)
If you ask them, ‘Who created the heavens and the earth?’ they will surely say, ‘Allah.’ Say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah!’ But most of them do not know. (25)

Key Points

A similar expression has also appeared in other instances in the Quran, including the verses: 61 and 63 of Al-'Ankabut, 38 of Az-Zumar, and 9 of Az-Zukhruf, which shows that the polytheists did not deny the Creatorship of Allah, and their deviation lay in associating idols [with Him] during worship and hoping for intercession from them, «و ما نعبدهم الاّ لیقرّبونا الى اللّه زُلفى »(323) even though they themselves had carved them from stone and wood. Imam Baqir عليه السلام considered the polytheists' answer in this verse to be an indication of the innate nature (Fitrah) of Tawhid (monotheism).(324)

323) Az-Zumar, 3. 324) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, concerning the verse, and Tawhid al-Saduq, p. 330.

Messages

1- The Creatorship of Allah is not hidden even from the polytheists. «و لئن سألتهم من خَلَق... لیقولنّ اللّه»

2- The polytheists implicitly acknowledge their deviation. «لئن سألتهم... لیقولنّ اللّه»

3- All creation belongs to Him, so let us praise Him alone. «قل الحمدللّه»

4- Express thanks to Allah verbally. «قل الحمدلِلّه»

5- The deviation of the polytheists usually stems from their ignorance. «بل اكثرهم لایعلمون»

Ayah

لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْغَنِىُّ ٱلْحَمِيدُ (۲٦)
To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Indeed Allah is the All-sufficient, the All-laudable. (26)

Messages

1- Allah is not in need of anyone, neither in creation nor in the administration of the universe. «خلق السّموات... للّه ما فى السّموات... هو الغنىّ»

2- Only Allah is the Creator and Owner of creation, «للّه ما فى السّموات و الارض» and only He is the Self-Sufficient (Need-less) and worthy of praise. «انّ اللّه هو الغنىّ الحمید»

3- The command to praise and glorify Allah in the previous Ayah is not due to His need. «قل الحمدللّه... هو الغنىّ الحمید»

Ayah

وَلَوْ أَنَّمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مِن شَجَرَةٍ أَقْلَـٰمٌ وَٱلْبَحْرُ يَمُدُّهُۥ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ سَبْعَةُ أَبْحُرٍ مَّا نَفِدَتْ كَلِمَـٰتُ ٱللَّهِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ حَكِيمٌ (۲۷)
If all the trees on the earth were pens, and the sea replenished with seven more seas [were ink], the words of Allah would not be spent. Indeed Allah is all-mighty, all-wise. (27)

Key Points

| We also read in Ayah 109 of Surah Al-Kahf: «قل لو كان البحر مداداً لكلمات ربّى لَنَفدَ البحر قبلَ أن تَنفد كلمات ربّى و لو جئنا بمِثله مَدَداً» Say: If the sea were ink for the Words of my Lord, the sea would surely be exhausted before the Words of my Lord were exhausted, even if We brought the like of it (the first sea) as an addition. Perhaps the intent of «سبعة ابحر» (seven seas) is abundance, and the number itself holds no specific significance. That is, even if the water of all the seas were made into ink, they still could not record all the Divine blessings.

Messages

1- The school of the Prophets detaches man from superficiality and limited vision, connecting him to the infinite. «و لو اَنّما... ما نفدت كلمات اللّه»

2- The Divine Words are uncountable. «ما نفدت كلمات اللّه»

What is the Word of Allah (كلمة اللّه)?

  1. The blessings of Allah. «قل لو كان البحر مداداً لكلمات ربّى لنفد البحر...»(325)

  2. The Divine traditions (Sunan). «و لقد سبقت كلمتنا لعبادنا المرسلین . انّهم لهم المنصورون»(326) and «ولولا كلمة الفصل لقضى بینهم»(327)

  3. The special creations of Allah. «انّما المسیح عیسى ابن مريم رسول اللّه و كلمته»(328)

  4. The events by which man is tested. «و اذا ابتلى ابراهیم ربّه بكلمات»(329)

  5. The Divine signs (Ayat). Regarding Lady Maryam, we read: «و صدّقت بكلمات ربّها»(330)

  6. The means by which truth triumphs over falsehood. «و یرید اللّه ان یحقّ الحق بكلماته و یقطع دابر الكافرین»(331), «و یمح اللّه الباطل و یحقّ الحق بكلماته»(332)

By compiling the preceding points, it becomes clear that the intent of «كلمة» (Word) is not merely a sound or a letter, but rather the Divine traditions, creations, Will, and favors that manifest in creation.

Therefore, if all the trees were pens and all the seas were ink, they could not write the Divine Words—meaning they could not record the creations of Allah, the Divine favors, and the traditions that have existed throughout history for mankind and all beings. «واللّه العالم»

In a narration, we read that Imam Kadhim عليه السلام said: "The manifestation of the Words of Allah is us, whose virtues are beyond comprehension and counting."(333)

Indeed, the friends of Allah (Awliya' Allah) are the intermediaries of grace (Wāsiṭat al-Fayḍ) from His Holy Essence, and every favor and perfection reaches others through those great personalities. 325) Kahf, 109. 326) Saffat, 171-172. 327) Shura, 21. 328) Nisa, 171. 329) Baqarah, 124. 330) Tahrim, 12. 331) Anfal, 7. 332) Shura, 24. 333) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, concerning the Ayah.

Ayah

مَّا خَلْقُكُمْ وَلَا بَعْثُكُمْ إِلَّا كَنَفْسٍ وَٰحِدَةٍ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَمِيعٌۢ بَصِيرٌ (۲۸)
Your creation and your resurrection are not but as of a single soul. Indeed Allah is all-hearing, all-seeing. (28)

Key Points

The root of skepticism and doubt concerning the principle of Resurrection (Ma'ad) sometimes relates to the length of time: how the dead will be brought back to life after a long period has passed. Sometimes it relates to the dead themselves: how decayed, scattered, and mixed bones will be separated. And sometimes it relates to the knowledge of actions, deeds, and thoughts: how, after the dead are recreated, the accounts of all these human beings will be settled? Allah answers all these doubts in this Ayah with one sentence: bringing all of you back to life is the same as creating a single person, and time has no effect on it. He hears and sees your whispers, knows all your behavior and deeds, and settles the account of everyone.

Messages

1- In Allah's knowledge and power, quantity and population, time and place, hidden and manifest, have no effect. «ما خلقكم و لابعثكم الاّ كنفس واحدة»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُولِجُ ٱلَّيْلَ فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَيُولِجُ ٱلنَّهَارَ فِى ٱلَّيْلِ وَسَخَّرَ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ كُلٌّ يَجْرِىٓ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ (۲۹)
Have you not regarded that Allah makes the night pass into the day and makes the day pass into the night; and He has disposed the sun and the moon, each moving for a specified term, and that Allah is well aware of what you do? (29)

Key Points

The gradual shortening and lengthening of the night and day plays an important role in the nurturing of creatures that require more darkness or light. In this Ayah, the shortening and lengthening of the night and day, and the subjugation of the sun and the moon by Allah, are mentioned. Then, the movement of each for a specified term is referenced, and finally, the end of the Ayah states: Allah is Aware of what you human beings do. This means that the purpose of all existence and the various transformations in the system of creation is the action of human beings; therefore, we must do what is pleasing to Allah.

The cloud, the wind, the moon, the sun, and the heavens are all at work, So that you may obtain a loaf of bread and not eat it heedlessly. All are bewildered and obedient for your sake; It would not be fair if you were not obedient.

Most of the addresses in the Quran to the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله are not limited to his person alone but include the general public. For example, it says to the Prophet: "If one or both of them reach old age in your presence, do not say 'Uff' to them and do not express annoyance with them" (334), while we know that the Prophet lost His parents in childhood and they were not alive to reach old age in His presence. In this Ayah too, although the address "ألم تر" (Have you not seen) is directed to the Prophet of Islam, the end of the Ayah, which states: "انّ اللّه بما تعملون خبیر" (Indeed, Allah is well-acquainted with what you do), shows that the Ayah includes the general public.

(334) Isra', 23.

Messages

1- One of the ways to know Allah is through studying nature and the secrets of existence. «ألم تر...»

2- We should not consider the shortening and lengthening of the night and day to be accidental; rather, it is a Divine act based on design and planning. «یولج...»

3- The sun also moves. «الشمس و القمر كلّ یجرى»

4- The movements of the celestial bodies are timed. «كلّ یجرى الى أجلٍ مسمّىً»

5- The changes in night and day and the subjugation of the sun and the moon are for the benefit and utilization of humankind. «انّ اللّه بما تعملون خبیر» (The actions and behavior of human beings must also be beneficial and purposeful).

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ وَأَنَّ مَا يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِ ٱلْبَـٰطِلُ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْكَبِيرُ (۳٠)
That is because Allah is the Reality, and whatever they invoke besides Him is nullity, and because Allah is the All-exalted, the All-great. (30)

Key Points

From Ayah 24 to Ayah 30, ten Divine attributes are discussed: Al-Ghani (the Self-Sufficient), Al-Hamid (the Praiseworthy), Al-Aziz (the Mighty), Al-Hakim (the Wise), As-Sami (the Hearer), Al-Basir (the Seer), Al-Khabir (the Aware), Al-Haqq (the Truth), Al-Ali (the High), and Al-Kabir (the Great). In every Ayah, a point is mentioned. For example, in Ayah 25, the Creatorship of Allah is discussed, "خلق السّموات..." In Ayah 27, Divine blessings are referred to, "مانفدت كلمات اللّه" and in Ayah 28, the absolute power of the Lord regarding the origin and return (resurrection) of man is stated. "ما خَلقُكم و لابَعثُكم" Only Allah and whatever is from Him or dependent upon Him is the Truth (Haqq), and whatever is other than Him or dependent upon other than Him is falsehood (Batil) and unstable.

Messages

1- The source of all of Allah's actions and attributes in creation is His permanence and stability. "ذلك بانّ اللّه هوالحقّ"

2- Truth (Haqq) and falsehood (Batil) are the criteria for choice, not imitation, propaganda, temptation, or threat. "ذلك بانّ اللّه هوالحقّ و اَنّ ما یدعون من دونه الباطل"

3- We must have clarity and decisiveness in stating the truth. (In this Ayah, the word "أنّ" (verily/indeed) is used three times, which is a sign of emphasis.)

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱلْفُلْكَ تَجْرِى فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ بِنِعْمَتِ ٱللَّهِ لِيُرِيَكُم مِّنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ شَكُورٍ (۳۱)
Have you not regarded that the ships sail at sea with Allah’s blessing, that He may show you some of His signs? There are indeed signs in that for every patient and grateful [servant]. (31)

Key Points

The movement of ships in the sea is the result of a collection of divine blessings, including: the movement of the wind, specific gravity, and the laws of water pressure which cause objects to float on it. Furthermore, the seas are natural, cost-free, and public routes that connect all parts of the earth to one another, and even now, despite human advancements in the aviation industry, most goods are transported by ship. In the Qur'an, sabr (patience) and shukr (gratitude) are mentioned side-by-side in numerous instances. Perhaps the secret behind this is that the matters that occur around a person are either according to his desire, which calls for gratitude (shukr), or contrary to his desire, which calls for patience (sabr). Therefore, a person must constantly be either in a state of patience or in a state of gratitude; otherwise, he will be in a state of ingratitude (kufran) and heedlessness.

Messages

1- In addition to the celestial signs (Ayat), we should also pay attention to the terrestrial signs. (In the previous verses, He referred to the sky, the moon, and the sun; in this verse, He refers to the earth, the sea, and the ship.) «اَلَم تَرَ أنّ الفلك...»

2- When inviting people to Allah and explaining His blessings, in propagation and guidance, we should use natural and accessible matters. «اَلَم تَرَ أنّ الفلك...»

3- We should not look at creation simply. «اَلَم تَرَ» Creation consists of divine signs (Ayat). «لیریكم من آیاته...»

4- The movement of the ship upon the water is one of the mysterious and profound divine blessings. «تجرى فى البحر بنعمت اللّه»

5- If we wish to perceive more signs of Allah's power and benefit more abundantly from His divine blessings, we must show greater patience. («صبّار», meaning abundant patience) And we must strive to make the best use of what has been given and always be grateful. («شكور», meaning abundant gratitude)

Ayah

وَإِذَا غَشِيَهُم مَّوْجٌ كَٱلظُّلَلِ دَعَوُا۟ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ ٱلدِّينَ فَلَمَّا نَجَّىٰهُمْ إِلَى ٱلْبَرِّ فَمِنْهُم مُّقْتَصِدٌ ۚ وَمَا يَجْحَدُ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ إِلَّا كُلُّ خَتَّارٍ كَفُورٍ (۳۲)
When waves cover them like awnings; they invoke Allah, putting exclusive faith in Him. But when He delivers them towards land, [only] some of them remain unswerving. No one will impugn Our signs except an ungrateful traitor. (32)

Key Points

“Ẓulal” is the plural of “Ẓullah,” meaning a cloud that casts a shadow, and it is often used in cases where an unpleasant matter is involved. “Muqtaṣid” means moderation in action, and fulfilling covenants and promises. The word “Khattār” is a superlative form (ṣīghah mubālaghah), meaning one who breaks covenants frequently. Since polytheists and sinners repeatedly turn toward Allah and make covenants during distress, but break their promises after the calamities subside, Allah has named them “Khattār.” The faith of some people is permanent and stable, but the faith of others is temporary and seasonal; meaning this group only turns to Allah when material means and resources are cut off. It is like emergency lighting that is sought only when all the main lights go out.

Messages

1- Man is inherently cognizant of Allah (God-knowing), but material means and resources act like a veil covering this innate nature (fiṭrah). The occurrence of accidents and dangers removes this veil. «غشیهم... دعوا اللّه»

2- Material prosperity is a cause of heedlessness, but dangers and hardships are causes of attention, supplication, sincerity, and the removal of arrogance. «اذاغشیهم... دعوُا اللّه...»

3- Cutting off hope from ordinary means is one of the ways to achieve sincerity (ikhlāṣ). «مخلصین له الدین»

4- For the friends of Allah (awliyā’), heedlessness is temporary, «اذا مسّهم طائف من الشّیطان تذكّروا»(335) but for some groups, sincerity is temporary. «اذا غشیهم... مخلصین»

5- Sincere supplication is likely to be answered. «دعوا اللّه مخلصین... نجّاهم»

6- Man possesses free will and choice. «فمنهم مقتصد» (Some remain on the path of truth after being rescued from difficulties, but others return to the path of deviation and polytheism, and this demonstrates the freedom of human will and choice.)

7- The prerequisite of faith is moderation. (Instead of saying: «فمنهم مؤمن», He said:) «فمنهم مقتصد»

8- Covenant-breaking and ingratitude lead man to disbelief (kufr). «و ما یجحد بآیاتنا الاّ كلّ ختّار كفور»

Sincerity (Ikhlāṣ)

Sincerity is when an action is 100% for Allah. To the extent that even if one percent or less of it is for other than Allah, the worship is invalid or questionable.

  • If we choose the place of prayer for other than Allah, for example, standing where people can see us or where a camera can film us.
  • If we choose the time of worship for other than Allah, for example, praying at the beginning of the time slot to attract people's attention.
  • If the form and appearance in which we pray is for other than Allah, for example, putting a cloak over our shoulders, bowing our neck, changing our voice, and having a goal other than the pleasure of Allah—in all these cases, the prayer is invalid, and due to hypocrisy (riyā’), we have also committed a sin.

In other words, sincerity is that we disregard carnal desires and wishes, tyrants (ṭāghūts), political inclinations, and the demands of this person or that person. Rather, our motivation should be only one thing: obedience to the command of Allah and the fulfillment of duty.

Truly, achieving sincerity is impossible without Divine aid. The Quran states in Ayah 32 of Sūrah Luqmān: When a wave covers them like clouds, they call upon Allah sincerely; but when He delivers them to the land, (only) some of them take the path of moderation (and remain faithful to their belief).

Ways to Acquire Sincerity

  1. Attention to the Knowledge and Power of Allah.

If we know that all honor, power, and sustenance are in His hands, we will never seek honor, power, or sustenance from other than Him.

If we pay attention that by the Will of Allah, creatures are created, and by His Will, everything is annihilated. If we know that He is both the Creator of means and the Annihilator of means—meaning He makes a dry tree the source of fresh dates for Hadrat Maryam (Mary) and turns the fire, which is a means of burning, into a garden for Hadrat Ibrāhīm (Abraham)—we will not resort to anyone other than Him.

Hundreds of verses and stories in the Quran invite people to witness the manifestation of Allah's Power, so that perhaps people will abandon others and turn sincerely toward Him.

  1. Attention to the Blessings of Sincerity.

The sincere person has only one goal, and that is the pleasure of Allah. And whoever’s goal is only the pleasure of Allah does not look for the encouragement of others, is not afraid of blame, does not fear loneliness, does not retreat from his path, never regrets, does not become resentful due to people's indifference, despair finds no way into him, and in traversing the path of truth, he does not care about majority or minority.

The Quran states: Sincere fighters do not care whether they kill the enemy of Allah or are martyred in the way of Allah. Imam Husayn عليه السلام, on the eve of his journey to Karbala, said: We are going to Karbala, whether we are martyred or victorious, the goal is the fulfillment of duty.

  1. Attention to the Favors of Allah.

Another way that brings us closer to sincerity is remembering the favors of Allah. Let us not forget that we did not exist; a sperm was made from dust and nutrients and placed in the darkness of the mother's womb, passing through the stages of development one after another, and was born as a complete human being. At that time, he knew nothing and had only one skill: sucking his mother's milk—a complete food that meets all the body's needs, mother's milk accompanied by maternal affection, a mother who was at his service twenty-four hours a day. Does any awakened conscience allow that after attaining all those blessings, power, and awareness, one should become subservient to others? Why should we sell ourselves to others who have neither a right over us nor have done us any favor?!

  1. Attention to the Will of Allah.

If we know that the hearts of people are in the hand of Allah and that He is the Changer of Hearts (Muqallib al-Qulūb), we will perform the work for Allah, and wherever we need public support, we will ask Allah to grant us the necessary love, popularity, and standing in the hearts of the people and in public opinion.

Hadrat Ibrāhīm (Abraham), in the hot and scorching deserts of Hijaz, raised the foundations of the Ka'bah and asked Allah to incline the hearts of people toward his progeny. Thousands of years have passed since this event, and every year millions of people passionately, more excitedly than butterflies, circumambulate that House.

How many individuals put themselves through great hardship to satisfy people, yet people still do not love them? And how many individuals, without expecting anything from people—material gains, fame, bread, or status—devote their hearts to Allah and sincerely fulfill their duty; yet, in the eyes of the people, they possess special greatness and dignity. Therefore, the goal must be the pleasure of Allah, and we should ask Allah for the pleasure of the people as well.

  1. Attention to the Permanence of the Deed.

When a deed is done for Allah, it remains permanent and enduring because it has taken on the color of Allah. If a deed is not for Allah, its expiration date will come sooner or later. The Quran states: «ما عندكم یَنفد و ما عنداللّه باق»(336) and no wise person prefers the transient over the permanent or deals with it.

  1. Comparing Rewards.

In the rewards given by people, there are various limitations. For example, if people want to reward a prophet, they provide him with the best clothing, food, and housing, whereas all these blessings are limited and are also available to unworthy individuals. Unworthy people can also use various adornments, palaces, gardens, and luxurious personal conveyances.

But if the deed is done for Allah, an infinite reward awaits, both material and spiritual rewards.

Here too, if we think correctly, reason does not allow us to exchange infinite and comprehensive rewards for limited human rewards.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ ٱتَّقُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ وَٱخْشَوْا۟ يَوْمًا لَّا يَجْزِى وَالِدٌ عَن وَلَدِهِۦ وَلَا مَوْلُودٌ هُوَ جَازٍ عَن وَالِدِهِۦ شَيْـًٔا ۚ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ ۖ فَلَا تَغُرَّنَّكُمُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَلَا يَغُرَّنَّكُم بِٱللَّهِ ٱلْغَرُورُ (۳۳)
O mankind! Be wary of your Lord and fear the day when a father will not atone for his child, nor the child will atone for its father in any wise. Indeed Allah’s promise is true. So do not let the life of the world deceive you, nor let the Deceiver deceive you concerning Allah. (33)

Key Points

The phrase "لایَجْزى" (Lā yajzī) means both recompense, punishment, and reward, and also sufficiency and taking responsibility. The second meaning is intended here. "غَرور" (Gharūr) is a form of intensive exaggeration (ṣīghah mubālaghah), meaning the great deceiver. Its clear manifestation is Satan, and it is applied to anything or anyone that deceives man.

Messages

1- Everyone must fear Allah and abstain from all types of doctrinal, moral, and practical deviations. «یا ایّها النّاس اتّقوا ربّكم»

2- The best provision for the Day when even a father and son cannot help one another is piety (Taqwa). «اتّقوا ربّكم و اخشوا یوماً لایجزى والد...»

3- The danger of the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) is serious; therefore, continuous warnings are necessary. (The command is given twice and the prohibition twice in the Ayah: «اتّقوا - اخشوا - لاتغرّنّكم - لایغرّنّكم»)

4- The Day of Judgment is a great day. (The word «یوماً» is indefinite and carries the tanwīn, which is a sign of its grandeur and greatness.) «اخشوا یوماً»

5- On the Day of Judgment, everyone is responsible for his own deeds and is not responsible for the deeds of another. «لایجزى... شیئاً»

6- What makes man heedless of the Day of Judgment is the deceptive world and Satan. «فلاتغرّنّكم الحیاة الدنیا و لایغرّنّكم باللّه الغرور»

7- Lineage and ancestry are ineffective on the Day of Judgment. «لایجزى والد - و لامولود» (When a father and son cannot help one another, the situation of others is clear.)

8- Fear of the Day of Judgment prevents man from being deceived by the life of this world. «اخشوا یوماً... و لایغرّنّكم باللّه الغرور»

9- Satan sometimes uses even the most sacred matters to deceive people. «و لایغرّنّكم باللّه الغرور»

The Day of Judgment, the Day of Solitude

Allah is Wise, and a Wise God does not destroy what He has created. The world is a house whose Engineer demolishes it in order to build a better structure. According to narrations, death is a change of clothing, a change of residence, and a prelude to perfection and eternal life.

Furthermore, the Justice of Allah necessitates the existence of the Day of Judgment for mankind, because we see good and bad people who do not receive their reward or punishment in this world. Therefore, the Just Allah must appoint another abode for their reward and punishment.

Although punishments and rewards are occasionally observed in this world, the place for many deeds is only the Day of Judgment, because in some cases, their realization is impossible in this world. For example, someone who has been martyred in the way of Allah is not present in this world to receive a reward, or someone who has killed many people cannot endure more than one punishment in this world.

Moreover, the suffering and punishment of the transgressor in this world also affects all his innocent relatives. Therefore, the reward and punishment must take place somewhere where it does not affect others.

The gathering of the scattered particles of the dead is also not an impossible task. Just as the fat particles scattered in a churn of buttermilk gather together due to the shaking of the churn, Allah also gathers the components of all the dead in one place by severely shaking the earth. «اذا زلزلت الارض زلزالها . و أخرجت الارض أثقالها»(337) Furthermore, we ourselves were created from a single cell, and this cell originated from particles of dust that turned into wheat, rice, vegetables, etc., becoming the food of the parents, then semen, and finally developing into a complete human being.

What is highlighted in this Ayah is the heartbreaking scene where a father cannot do anything for his child. In other instances in the Quran, scenes are presented which, if we look at them together, will make us realize the loneliness, humiliation, and terror of man:

On that Day, neither wealth nor sons will avail the disbelieving man. «یوم لاینفع مال و لابنون»(338)

On that Day, the intimate friends of the disbelievers will not ask about one another. «و لایسئل حمیم حمیاً»(339)

On that Day, regret and apology will not benefit the disbeliever. «و لایؤذن لهم فیعتذرون»(340)

On that Day, relationships and acquaintances will not benefit the disbeliever. «فلاانساب بینهم یومئذ»(341)

On that Day, the means and connections for the disbeliever will be cut off. «تَقطّعت بهم الاسباب»(342)

In any case, that Day is the day of man's alienation and solitude. (337) Al-Zalzalah, 1 - 2. (338) Ash-Shu'ara, 88. (339) Al-Ma'arij, 10. (340) Al-Mursalat, 36. (341) Al-Mu'minun, 101. (342) Al-Baqarah, 166.

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عِندَهُۥ عِلْمُ ٱلسَّاعَةِ وَيُنَزِّلُ ٱلْغَيْثَ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلْأَرْحَامِ ۖ وَمَا تَدْرِى نَفْسٌ مَّاذَا تَكْسِبُ غَدًا ۖ وَمَا تَدْرِى نَفْسٌۢ بِأَىِّ أَرْضٍ تَمُوتُ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ خَبِيرٌۢ (۳٤)
Indeed the knowledge of the Hour is with Allah. He sends down the rain, and He knows what is in the wombs. No soul knows what it will earn tomorrow, and no soul knows in what land it will die. Indeed Allah is all-knowing, all-aware. (34)

Key Points

Knowledge of the time of death and the Hour (Qiyamah) belongs exclusively to Allah. If humans knew that their death was far off and the Hour would not be established soon, they would become arrogant and indulge more in sin. And if they knew that the Hour would be established soon, they would panic and cease work and activity. Therefore, since we do not know the time of death and the Hour, we must always be prepared. Knowledge of the infants in the mothers' wombs belongs exclusively to Allah. Although today's scientific devices and tests show whether the fetus is male or female, Allah's knowledge is eternal and unlimited. Furthermore, the knowledge of "ما فى الارحام" (what is in the wombs) is not only related to the gender of the fetus, but also includes talents, conditions, temperaments, and hundreds of other pieces of knowledge that are unattainable by any human device, test, or means. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «عرفتُ اللّه بِفَسخِ العَزائم و حلّ العقود»(343), "I recognized Allah through the breaking of firm resolves and the undoing of contracts (decisions)." Imam Ali عليه السلام commented on the Ayah, saying: «مِن قَدَم الى قَدَم»(344), meaning that when a person takes one step, he does not know about his next step.

343) Nahj al-Balaghah, Hikmah 250. 344) Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 139.

Messages

1- Human knowledge is limited and cannot be compared to the infinite knowledge of Allah. «یعلم... ما تدرى نفس»

2- Planning, management, and organizing affairs are necessary; but there is a superior power at work, and man does not know what will happen tomorrow. «و ما تدرى نفس ماذا تكسب غداً»

3- No one is aware of the time and place of his own death. «و ما تدرى نفس بأى ارض تموت» (If we read in narrations that a certain friend of Allah (Wali) knew the place or time of his demise or martyrdom, that knowledge was granted to him by Allah; otherwise, independently, no one has this information.)

4- We should not rely on the predictions of soothsayers, fortune-tellers, palm readers, and prognosticators regarding our future. «و ما تدرى نفس ماذا تكسب غداً»

5- Why is a human being, who knows neither his death nor his situation tomorrow, arrogant? «ما تدرى نفس...»

6- Man must always be ready to appear (on the scene) and be accountable. «و ما تدرى نفس باىّ ارض تموت»

O Lord! Make the Quran our light, our law, our hope, our logic, the source of our intellect and contemplation, our admonition, our healing, our tranquility, and our salvation.
